dataset.name: CBIS-DDSM (Curated Breast Imaging Subset of DDSM)
dataset.description: CBIS-DDSM is a curated and standardized subset of the Digital Database for Screening Mammography (DDSM). It provides decompressed mammography images converted to DICOM, updated ROI segmentation for mass lesions, bounding boxes, pathology labels, BI-RADS assessments, and predefined training/test splits to support reproducible CADx and CADe research.
dataset.authors: Rebecca Sawyer Lee; Francisco Gimenez; Assaf Hoogi; Kanae Kawai Miyake; Mia Gorovoy; Daniel L. Rubin

contact.institution: Stanford University School of Medicine, Department of Radiology and Biomedical Data Science

dataset.license: Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported (CC BY 3.0) via TCIA Data Usage Policy

data_description.description: The dataset contains full-field mammograms and abnormality-level crops. It includes 753 calcification cases and 891 mass cases, split into standardized training and test sets (80/20). Mass lesions include refined segmentation masks generated using a modified Chan-Vese level-set algorithm.

data_collection.source: Curated subset of the Digital Database for Screening Mammography (DDSM), originally collected from Massachusetts General Hospital, Wake Forest University School of Medicine, Sacred Heart Hospital, and Washington University of St Louis School of Medicine
data_collection.equipment: Scanned film mammograms digitized using multiple scanners; converted to standardized optical density and DICOM format
data_collection.collection_period: Original DDSM released 1997; CBIS-DDSM curated and published 2016
data_collection.annotation_process: Original DDSM ROIs were reviewed; 339 questionable mass images removed. Mass lesions were re-segmented using a modified Chan-Vese local level-set algorithm initialized from original contours. A trained mammographer reviewed annotations and provided hand-drawn ROIs for validation (118 images).

images.description: Full mammograms (CC and MLO views) and cropped abnormality images

annotation_fields.name: patient_id
annotation_fields.description: Unique participant identifier (first 7 characters of image file)

annotation_fields.name: abnormality id
annotation_fields.description: index of lesion in sigle image

annotation_fields.name: pathology
annotation_fields.description: Benign, Benign without callback, or Malignant

inter_annotator_agreement.description: Mass segmentation validated on 118 images. Mean Dice coefficient between computer-generated and hand-drawn ROIs: 0.792±0.108; original DDSM vs hand-drawn: 0.398±0.195. Wilcoxon signed-rank test p=5.54e-19, indicating significant improvement.

quality_and_validation.data_imbalance: Benign and malignant cases are moderately balanced within mass and calcification subsets; official train/test split preserves BI-RADS stratification.
quality_and_validation.preprocessing: Lossless LJPEG decompression; optical density normalization; clipping to [0.05, 3.0]; conversion to 16-bit TIFF and DICOM; ROI mask generation; abnormality cropping.
quality_and_validation.limitations: Scanned film mammograms rather than full-field digital mammography; heterogeneous scanner sources; incomplete density/BIRADS counts in summary; some cases contain multiple patient IDs per participant.

privacy_and_ethics.anonymization: All images de-identified and reviewed; cases with residual PHI removed by TCIA.
privacy_and_ethics.consent: Original DDSM obtained necessary patient consents under DoD Breast Cancer Research Program.
privacy_and_ethics.access_control: Public access via The Cancer Imaging Archive (TCIA) under data usage agreement.

access_and_usage.access_method: Download via TCIA (The Cancer Imaging Archive) using DOI link and JNLP files.
access_and_usage.updates: Curated release published 2016; no major subsequent revisions documented.

references.title: Curated Breast Imaging Subset of DDSM
references.authors: Lee RS; Gimenez F; Hoogi A; Rubin DL

references.title: A curated mammography data set for use in computer-aided detection and diagnosis research
references.authors: Lee RS; Gimenez F; Hoogi A; Miyake KK; Gorovoy M; Rubin DL
